attributes.content:
## Overview

The Mileage Log card gives the workspace a structured home for trip mileage tracker: date, distance, purpose, start/end location. For tax/expense reporting.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Mileage Log acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Calendar]]** -- Calendar can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Mileage Log.
- **[[Task]]** -- Mileage Log often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Mileage Log.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Mileage Log can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Mileage Log can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Recipe]]** -- Recipe can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Mileage Log.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Mileage Log as the structured home for trip mileage tracker: date, distance, purpose, start/end location. For tax/expense reporting and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Mileage Log when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Mileage Log stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
